introSection: Turbo is a Pinfari steel roller coaster located at Brighton Palace Pier in Brighton, East Sussex, England, UK.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
This roller coaster opened in 1996. It would have been called "Zyklon" however the name was changed following complaints from Jewish people. A "Zyklon" sign above the loop had to be removed prior to opening.

On 4 February 2003, Turbo was damaged in a fire. It remained closed for about two months for repairs. In early 2013, Turbo was taken down and sent away to be renovated. It was open again by late March.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Before its renovation, the Turbo had yellow track and white supports. It has blue track and yellow supports nowadays.

fullText:
Turbo is a Pinfari steel roller coaster located at Brighton Palace Pier in Brighton, East Sussex, England, UK.

This roller coaster opened in 1996. It would have been called "Zyklon" however the name was changed following complaints from Jewish people. A "Zyklon" sign above the loop had to be removed prior to opening.

On 4 February 2003, Turbo was damaged in a fire. It remained closed for about two months for repairs. In early 2013, Turbo was taken down and sent away to be renovated. It was open again by late March.



Before its renovation, the Turbo had yellow track and white supports. It has blue track and yellow supports nowadays.
